content: Self-Refine's iterative single-model self-feedback loop can be structurally augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to solve the 'blind spot' problem of self-feedback that never challenges its own initial assumptions. This creates a new zero-training refinement framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra supervised data while avoiding the stagnation that comes from a single model iteratively refining its own unexamined bias.
